title: Agent SDK Runtime — live through the orchestrator

segments.subtitle: agent-garrison · the orchestrator routes per task: Ollama via the Agent SDK vs Claude on the Max plan · 2026-06-17

beats.caption: The orchestrator's runtime selection (profile: demo) — fast work → agent-sdk/ollama, expert work → claude-code/anthropic
beats.expectedScreen: the Model Router 'Role → target (demo)' table: the fast row's target is sdk-ollama-chat and the expert row's is cc-opus-high, with an 'ollama-local' badge in the header

beats.caption: Web channel → a trivial question. The orchestrator will route this to the local Ollama model via the Agent SDK.
beats.expectedScreen: the Operative web-channel chat with 'What is the capital of France?' typed into the composer, ready to send

actions.text: What is the capital of France? Answer in one short sentence.

beats.caption: Ollama (qwen3:0.6b) answers via the Agent SDK runtime — the status badge reads agent-sdk/ollama-local
beats.expectedScreen: the chat showing the assistant reply 'The capital of France is Paris.' and a status badge reading 'qwen3:0.6b · agent-sdk/ollama-local'

beats.caption: A harder question in the SAME channel — the orchestrator will route this one to Claude on the Max plan
beats.expectedScreen: the chat composer with the database-locking question typed in; the previous 'Paris' reply still visible above

actions.text: Explain in 3 sentences the key trade-offs between optimistic and pessimistic locking in databases.

beats.caption: Claude (Opus) answers via the claude-code PTY runtime — same channel, a different runtime chosen per task
beats.expectedScreen: the chat showing a substantive Claude answer about optimistic vs pessimistic locking (mentioning conflicts / commit time / deadlocks)

segments.caption: The orchestrator's decision log — which runtime and model handled each turn
segments.expectedScreen: terminal output listing the recent decisions: role=fast -> sdk-ollama-chat runtime=agent-sdk provider=ollama-local model=qwen3:0.6b, and role=expert -> cc-opus-high runtime=claude-code provider=anthropic-plan model=opus

segments.text: One channel, one orchestrator, two runtimes
segments.subtitle: trivial → Ollama via the Agent SDK (fenced, non-Anthropic) · hard → Claude on the Max plan · runtime+model logged per turn
